status_notes: Both documents are freely available in English with no bot-block on cyber.go.jp, but their PDFs use GPKI digital-signature/encryption that resisted this pass's automated text extraction — a real browser/PDF reader opens them fine. No structured control-level extraction yet.
notes: See also registry/regulation/jp/go/cyber.json (Basic Act on Cybersecurity, Active Cyber Defense Act) and registry/entity/jp/go/cyber.json (the organization itself) for other type-specific files under this namespace.

match_nodes.description: Common Standards for Cybersecurity Measures for Government Agencies and Related Agencies — the government-agency security baseline

data.official_name: Common Standards for Cybersecurity Measures for Government Agencies and Related Agencies
data.notes: Issued by the Cybersecurity Strategic Headquarters under Article 26(1) of the Basic Act on Cybersecurity (secid:regulation/cyber.go.jp/basic-act-cybersecurity). Most recently revised June 27, 2025 (FY2025/Reiwa 7 edition). Structured as Chapter 1 General Provisions (purpose, scope, revision process, compliance) followed by a Basic Framework of Information Security Measures covering organizational structure/promotion systems, asset/ledger management, risk assessment, incident response systems, and agency-specific standards. Recent amendments added cloud (ISMAP-aligned) controls, mobile-device access policy, and CDN/EDR provisions. Japan's baseline/mandatory information-security control set for all central government ministries and incorporated administrative agencies — analogous to a national government security baseline (comparable in role to NIST SP 800-53 for the US federal government).

urls.note: Current (FY2025/R7) full text, English. Freely accessible; GPKI-signed PDF resisted automated text extraction.

match_nodes.description: Cybersecurity Policy for Critical Infrastructure Protection — defines Japan's critical infrastructure sectors

data.official_name: Cybersecurity Policy for Critical Infrastructure Protection
data.notes: Issued by the Cybersecurity Strategic Headquarters; current edition dated 2024 (revision path running from June 2022). Designates roughly 14-15 critical infrastructure sectors: information & communications, finance, aviation, airports, railways, electric power, gas, government/administrative services, medical, water, logistics, chemical, credit cards, petroleum, and (added in a recent revision) ports/harbours. Japan's sector-designation document, equivalent in role to the CISA 16-sector list in the US, and the umbrella policy under which the sector-specific safety guidelines in registry/control/jp/go/mlit.json and registry/control/jp/go/mhlw.json operate.

urls.note: Current (2024) full text, English. Freely accessible; GPKI-signed PDF resisted automated text extraction.
